claim: An agent is a system using an LLM to reason and decide when to take actions (tool calls) and observe outcomes, creating a loop—the LLM determines action count, not predefined steps
actionable: Design agent systems where the LLM autonomously decides how many actions to take based on reasoning about task completion.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Build agents for complex decision-making around tool interaction and gray-area logic; use workflows for stable, predictable automations to avoid overengineering
actionable: Before building an agent, ask: is this task unpredictable or does it require complex reasoning? If stable logic suffices, use simpler automation.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Four components define any agent: LLM (reasoning), tools (environment interaction), instructions/system prompt (behavior), and memory (short and long-term context)
actionable: When troubleshooting agent failures, diagnose which of these four components is failing: insufficient LLM reasoning, poor tools, vague instructions, or missing memory.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: React (reason-act-observe) is the standard pattern for agents—the LLM reasons about what to do, takes action, observes results, reflects, and iterates
actionable: Structure agent prompts and execution loops to follow ReAct explicitly—reason first, then act, then embed observation feedback.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Common agent patterns: prompt chaining (sequential LLM calls), routing (router LLM selects specialist agent), tool use, evaluator loops (LLM produces, LLM evaluates), orchestrator-worker, and autonomous loops
actionable: Choose agent architecture based on task complexity—single agents for simple tasks, routers for multiple domains, orchestrator-worker for decomposable problems.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Limit single agents to 10-15 tools; beyond that, split into multi-agent systems with routing or orchestrator-worker patterns to prevent tool overload and confusion
actionable: When your single agent exceeds 10-15 tools, refactor into multiple specialized agents with a router or manager.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Building agents is 25% coding, 75% evaluation—start simple, add visibility into agent reasoning, write clear instructions, constantly evaluate and tweak tools/prompts/knowledge
actionable: Invest heavily in evals and tracing; expect to iterate on prompts, tools, and system design based on real-world performance data.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: All LLMs hallucinate—guardrails prevent hallucinations from corrupting agent systems: limit actions, require human review, filter outputs, test in safe environments first
actionable: Implement guardrails matching your risk profile: read-only tools for sensitive data, human review for high-stakes actions, relevance filters for RAG, and staging environments.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Real-world agent use cases: customer service (classify and respond), business operations (approve refunds, review docs, organize files), research, coding, and scheduling
actionable: Prioritize agent projects with clear ROI in customer service, document review, code generation, and calendar management.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes

claim: Focus agent development on outcomes, not complexity—don't build fancy multi-agent systems unless ROI justifies it; start simple and measure results
actionable: Before adding orchestrators, evaluators, or multi-agent complexity, measure whether a simpler agent solves the problem sufficiently.
source_title: Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI's Guides to AI Agents ALL in 18 Minutes
